CITY OF MILLBROOK
OFFICE OF THE CITY CLERK
PUBLIC RECORDS DIVISION

BUNDLE ID: ZBA-2024-06-01
SUBJECT: REGULAR MEETING MINUTES AND SUPPORTING DOCUMENTATION
DATE: JUNE 1, 2024
PAGE COUNT: 12 PAGES
STATUS: OFFICIAL RECORD

DOCUMENT COVER SHEET

This compilation contains public records from the Millbrook Zoning Board of Appeals regarding the Regular Meeting held on June 1, 2024. The following documents are included in this official bundle for distribution to all Board Members, City Clerk, and Public Record archives. The documents include the official meeting minutes, inter-departmental memoranda regarding logistics, campaign finance filings, property transfer records, environmental review summaries, and appendices containing public comment transcripts and staff reports. All documents are filed under the record date of June 1, 2024. The filing date for this bundle is June 1, 2024. The distribution list includes all Board Members, City Clerk, and relevant municipal departments.

The Zoning Board of Appeals convened at 6:00 PM. The meeting was called to order by Chairperson Patterson at 6:02 PM. A roll call was taken to establish a quorum. The meeting took place at City Hall Room 204. The ZBA Members present included Marcus Patterson, Elena Kowalski, David Rivera, Sarah Chen, Robert Thompson, and Linda Martinez. Planning Staff included Director James Wilson and Associate Planner Lisa Green. The City Clerk was John Davis. The meeting was recorded by the ZBA Administrative Assistant.

The following sections comprise the full administrative record for this session.

***

INTER-DEPARTMENTAL MEMORANDUM

TO: All Board Members, City Clerk
FROM: Millbrook Planning Department
DATE: May 28, 2024
SUBJECT: Logistics and Agenda Preparation for June 1, 2024 ZBA Meeting

This memorandum serves to confirm the logistical arrangements for the upcoming Regular Meeting of the Zoning Board of Appeals scheduled for June 1, 2024. The meeting location is confirmed as City Hall Room 204. The session is scheduled to begin at 6:00 PM. The agenda has been finalized and distributed to all Board Members and the City Clerk.

The agenda items are listed as follows:
Agenda Item 1: Variance 24-001
Agenda Item 2: Zoning Ordinance Amendment 24-02
Agenda Item 3: Public Comment

The Millbrook Planning Department has prepared the necessary materials for the hearing. Staff Report documentation is available for review. Site Plan documentation is available for review. The applicant, Thompson Residential LLC, has been notified of the hearing time and location. Public notification has been posted in accordance with municipal code requirements. The notice was posted at 1240 Oak Street.

The City Clerk, John Davis, is responsible for the official recording of the minutes. The ZBA Administrative Assistant is responsible for the distribution of the official record. The meeting is expected to adjourn by 6:45 PM. The next scheduled meeting is June 15, 2024.

Please review the attached Staff Report prior to the meeting. The Planning Director, James Wilson, is available to answer questions regarding the variance request. Associate Planner Lisa Green is available to provide technical specifications regarding the property lines.

This memorandum is part of the official administrative correspondence for the ZBA-2024-06-01 bundle. No further action is required from Board Members prior to the meeting. The City Clerk will maintain the distribution list. All Board Members are requested to review the documentation at least 24 hours prior to the convening of the board.

The Millbrook Planning Department maintains all records in compliance with state open meeting laws. The meeting location, City Hall Room 204, is accessible to the public. Seating is available for public comment. The public comment duration is set for 5 minutes per speaker. The number of speakers is limited to ensure the meeting concludes within the allotted time.

The administrative assistant will record the roll call. The roll call will verify the presence of a quorum. The quorum is established when a majority of the members are present. The ZBA Members present for the roll call are Marcus Patterson, Elena Kowalski, David Rivera, Sarah Chen, Robert Thompson, and Linda Martinez.

This memorandum is filed under the subject of Regular Meeting Minutes - June 1, 2024. The document status is Official Record. The page count for this memorandum is one page. The filing date is June 1, 2024. The distribution list includes all Board Members and City Clerk.

***

4. Traffic Patterns: No significant impact.
5. Wildlife Habitat: No significant impact.

REVIEW PROCESS
The review was conducted in accordance with the National Environmental Policy Act. The review was completed on June 1, 2024. The reviewer was Associate Planner Lisa Green. The reviewer was Director James Wilson. The review was filed with the City Clerk. The City Clerk is John Davis. The review was distributed to all Board Members. The distribution list includes all Board Members, City Clerk. The document status is Official Record. The page count for this summary is 2 pages. The attachment includes the site plan.

The variance type is Side Setback 2.5 feet. The setback variance is within standard tolerance. The Planning Director confirmed that the setback variance is within standard tolerance. The meeting was called to order by Chairperson Patterson at 6:02 PM. The board convened at 6:00 PM. The roll call was taken. The quorum was established.

AGENDA ITEM 1: VARIANCE 24-001
The Board considered the variance request for Parcel ID: 14-05-22-100. The address is 1240 Oak Street. The applicant is Thompson Residential LLC. The variance type is Side Setback 2.5 feet. The Staff Recommendation is APPROVE.

The Planning Director, James Wilson, presented the staff report. The Planning Director confirmed that the setback variance is within standard tolerance. Member Wilson noted that the applicant provided all required documentation. The applicant, Thompson Residential LLC, was present. Robert Thompson was present.

The Board reviewed the Site Plan. The Site Plan is Attachment 1. The Staff Report is Attachment 2. The Sign-in Sheet is Attachment 3. The Filing date is June 1, 2024. The Distribution list includes all Board Members, City Clerk. The Document status is Official Record. The Page count is 12 pages.

Discussion:
Chairperson Patterson asked for clarification on the side setback measurement. Associate Planner Lisa Green provided the measurement. The measurement was 2.5 feet. The standard tolerance was reviewed. The standard tolerance was met. The Millbrook Planning Department reviewed the property lines. The property lines were verified. The property lines match the legal description. The legal description is Lot 4, Block 12, Oak Street Subdivision.

Motion:
A motion was made to approve variance 24-001. The motion was made by Member Rivera. The motion was seconded by Member Chen. The motion was to approve. The motion was carried.

Vote:
The vote was recorded. The vote tally was 6 for, 0 against, 1 abstention. The motion to approve variance 24-001 was carried 6-0.

AGENDA ITEM 2: ZONING ORDINANCE AMENDMENT 24-02

The amendment relates to the square footage. The amendment relates to the lot size. The amendment relates to the zoning classification.

The ordinance text was distributed to the City Clerk. The City Clerk is John Davis. The City Clerk distributed the ordinance text.

Discussion:
Member Kowalski asked for clarification on the zoning classification. Member Kowalski asked for clarification on the R-1 Residential classification. The Planning Director provided the clarification. The Planning Director confirmed the classification. The classification is R-1 Residential. The classification is accurate. The classification is within standard tolerance.

Member Martinez asked for clarification on the square footage. Member Martinez asked for clarification on the lot size. The Associate Planner provided the clarification. The Associate Planner confirmed the square footage. The Associate Planner confirmed the lot size. The square footage is 7,500 square feet. The lot size is 0.17 acres.

Motion:
A motion was made to approve Zoning Ordinance Amendment 24-02. The motion was made by Member Thompson. The motion was seconded by Member Patterson. The motion was to approve. The motion was carried.

Vote:
The vote was recorded. The vote tally was 6 for, 0 against, 1 abstention. The motion to approve Zoning Ordinance Amendment 24-02 was carried 6-0.

ADJOURNMENT

The next scheduled meeting is open to the public. The next scheduled meeting is recorded by the ZBA Administrative Assistant.

ITEM 7: ADVERTISEMENT FOR BIDS – DOWNTOWN STREET RESURFACING PROJECT

Planning Director Elena R. Rossi presented the proposal for the Downtown Street Resurfacing Project. The project encompasses the resurfacing of Main Street from 1st Avenue to 5th Avenue, including the installation of new curb ramps, repainting of crosswalks, and the replacement of three sections of storm drain grates. The total estimated cost is $425,000. The funding source is the State Transportation Improvement Grant (STIG) 2023-2024, with a local match of 10%.

Staff Recommendation: The Planning Department recommends that the City Council authorize the advertisement for bids for the Downtown Street Resurfacing Project. The advertisement will be published in the Millbrook Gazette and the Millbrook Daily Chronicle for a period of two weeks. Bids will be accepted until 2:00 P.M. on July 15, 2024. The contract will be awarded to the lowest responsible bidder, subject to final engineering review and approval by the City Attorney.

Public Comment:
Council Member Miller asked if the project timeline accounts for the upcoming summer festival on Main Street.
Director Rossi responded that the project schedule has been coordinated with the Festival Committee. Construction is scheduled to begin on August 1, 2024, immediately following the conclusion of the festival.
Council Member O'Malley inquired about the material specifications for the asphalt mix.
Director Rossi stated that the specifications call for a polymer-modified asphalt mix to ensure durability and reduce noise levels, in compliance with the State Department of Transportation standards.
Council Member Thompson asked about the contingency fund allocation.
Director Rossi noted that the bid estimate includes a 5% contingency fund, which is standard for municipal infrastructure projects of this scope.

Motion by Council Member Chen, seconded by Council Member O'Malley, to authorize the advertisement for bids for the Downtown Street Resurfacing Project, Contract No. 2024-045, with a total estimated cost not to exceed $425,000, subject to final engineering review.

Roll Call Vote:
Council Member Vance: Aye
Council Member Chen: Aye
Council Member Miller: Aye
Council Member O'Malley: Aye
Council Member Thompson: Aye

Motion Carried.

REGULAR AGENDA ITEMS

ITEM 11: ZONING BOARD OF APPEALS HEARING – VARIANCE REQUEST FOR 450 RIVER ROAD

Planning Director Elena R. Rossi introduced the matter regarding a variance request submitted by the owner of 450 River Road, a property currently zoned R-2 (Single Family Residential). The applicant, Mr. James P. Henderson, is seeking a variance to allow the construction of a two-story accessory dwelling unit (ADU) and a detached garage structure that exceeds the current setback requirements by 4.5 feet. The proposed structure is to be used for short-term rental purposes, subject to the City's short-term rental ordinance.

The applicant states that the property is located in a high-demand area for short-term rentals due to its proximity to the Millbrook River Trail. The applicant argues that the variance is necessary to maximize the utility of the property and that the structure will not negatively impact the character of the neighborhood. The applicant has submitted a traffic impact study, an architectural rendering, and a letter of support from the immediate neighbors.

Staff Recommendation: The Planning Department recommends the denial of the variance request. The staff report notes that the proposed setback variance of 4.5 feet does not meet the criteria for a variance under Section 14.05 of the Millbrook Zoning Ordinance. The staff argues that the property is not unique in its physical characteristics and that the hardship is self-imposed. Furthermore, the staff notes that the proposed use as a short-term rental may create a nuisance for the surrounding residential area.

Zoning Board Administrator Marcus T. Webb stated that the Zoning Board of Appeals will hear the case on June 18, 2024. The Board will review the application, hear testimony from the applicant and the public, and render a decision. The Board may approve the variance with conditions, deny the variance, or remand the application for further review.

Public Comment:
Council Member Vance asked if the applicant has addressed the noise concerns raised in the traffic impact study.
Director Rossi responded that the applicant has proposed a sound barrier wall along the property line, which is included in the architectural rendering.
Council Member O'Malley asked if the short-term rental license has been issued.
Director Rossi stated that the short-term rental license has not been issued as the variance is a prerequisite for the license.
Council Member Thompson asked if the neighbors have signed the letter of support.
Director Rossi confirmed that the letter of support was signed by the owners of 448 and 452 River Road.

Motion by Council Member Chen, seconded by Council Member Miller, to forward the variance request for 450 River Road to the Zoning Board of Appeals for a hearing on June 18, 2024, and to direct the Planning Department to prepare a comprehensive staff report for the hearing.

Roll Call Vote:
Council Member Vance: Aye
Council Member Chen: Aye
Council Member Miller: Aye
Council Member O'Malley: Aye
Council Member Thompson: Aye

Motion Carried.

ITEM 12: ENVIRONMENTAL REVIEW SUMMARY – MILLBROOK WASTEWATER TREATMENT PLANT UPGRADE

Public Works Director James P. Garris presented the Environmental Review Summary for the Millbrook Wastewater Treatment Plant Upgrade Project. The project involves the installation of new filtration systems, the expansion of the sludge dewatering facility, and the upgrade of the disinfection system to comply with the new State Environmental Protection Standards. The total estimated cost is $2,850,000. The funding source is a combination of State Environmental Improvement Grants and City General Fund reserves.

The Environmental Impact Statement (EIS) was completed on May 15, 2024. The EIS concluded that the project would have a minimal impact on the local ecosystem. The primary concerns addressed in the EIS were potential air quality impacts during construction and the disposal of sludge. The EIS recommends the implementation of dust control measures and the use of a certified waste disposal facility for sludge.

Staff Recommendation: The Public Works Department recommends the approval of the Environmental Review Summary and the authorization to proceed with the design phase of the project. The design phase is expected to take six months. Construction is scheduled to begin in January 2025.

Public Comment:
Council Member Miller asked about the timeline for the sludge disposal facility certification.
Director Garris responded that the certification process is underway and is expected to be completed by August 2024.
Council Member O'Malley asked if the dust control measures have been budgeted.
Director Garris confirmed that dust control measures are included in the construction budget.
Council Member Thompson asked about the public notification process for the EIS.
Director Garris stated that the EIS was published in the Millbrook Gazette and available for public review at the City Library and the Public Works Department office for a period of 30 days. No public comments were received during the review period.

Motion by Council Member Chen, seconded by Council Member O'Malley, to approve the Environmental Review Summary for the Millbrook Wastewater Treatment Plant Upgrade Project and to authorize the design phase.

Roll Call Vote:
Council Member Vance: Aye
Council Member Chen: Aye
Council Member Miller: Aye
Council Member O'Malley: Aye
Council Member Thompson: Aye

Motion Carried.

ITEM 14: PUBLIC COMMENT SUBMISSIONS – ZONING AND PLANNING

The City Clerk opened the public comment period for zoning and planning matters. The following submissions were received:

1. Submission from John D. Smith, resident of 450 River Road, regarding the variance request for the property. Mr. Smith expressed concern about the potential increase in traffic and noise. He requested that the Zoning Board of Appeals deny the variance.
2. Submission from the Millbrook Neighborhood Association, regarding the downtown street resurfacing project. The Association expressed support for the project but requested that the construction schedule be adjusted to minimize disruption to local businesses.
3. Submission from Green Valley Farms, regarding the contribution to the Rivera Campaign Fund. The submission clarified that the contribution was a standard donation and not related to any specific zoning application.
4. Submission from Henderson Construction, regarding the contribution to the Patterson Campaign Committee. The submission stated that the contribution was made in accordance with the City's campaign finance laws and did not influence any council decisions.
5. Submission from Local Bank & Trust, regarding the contribution to the Kowalski for ZBA committee. The submission confirmed that the contribution was a voluntary donation and did not create a conflict of interest.

Council Member Miller asked if the submissions from the campaign finance donors were reviewed by the Ethics Commission.
City Clerk Halloway responded that the submissions were reviewed and are part of the public record. The Ethics Commission confirmed that no conflicts of interest were reported.
Council Member O'Malley asked if the submissions from the residents were addressed in the staff reports.
Director Rossi responded that the concerns raised by Mr. Smith and the Neighborhood Association were included in the staff reports for the respective hearings.

Public Comment Closed.

ITEM 15: INTER-DEPARTMENTAL MEMO – COORDINATION OF SUMMER EVENTS

City Manager Richard D. Sterling presented an Inter-Departmental Memo regarding the coordination of summer events in Millbrook. The memo outlines the responsibilities of various departments for the upcoming summer festival, the farmers market, and the outdoor concert series.

The memo details the following:
- Public Works Department: Responsible for setting up stages, lighting, and sound equipment.
- Police Department: Responsible for security, traffic control, and emergency response.
- Parks and Recreation Department: Responsible for vendor coordination, food service, and activity planning.
- Planning Department: Responsible for permit issuance and zoning compliance.
- Finance Department: Responsible for budget oversight and revenue collection.
- Communications Department: Responsible for public relations and media outreach.

The memo emphasizes the need for close coordination between departments to ensure the success of the events. A joint planning meeting is scheduled for June 10, 2024, to finalize the details.

Council Member Chen asked about the budget allocation for the summer events.
City Manager Sterling responded that the budget for the summer events is $150,000, which is funded by the City's Special Events Fund.
Council Member O'Malley asked about the contingency plan for inclement weather.
City Manager Sterling stated that the contingency plan includes the availability of indoor venues and the postponement of events in case of severe weather.
Council Member Thompson asked about the vendor selection process.
City Manager Sterling responded that vendors are selected through a competitive bidding process, with priority given to local businesses.

Motion by Council Member Chen, seconded by Council Member Miller, to approve the Inter-Departmental Memo regarding the coordination of summer events and to schedule the joint planning meeting for June 10, 2024.

Roll Call Vote:
Council Member Vance: Aye
Council Member Chen: Aye
Council Member Miller: Aye
Council Member O'Malley: Aye
Council Member Thompson: Aye

Motion Carried.

ITEM 2: VARIANCE REQUEST FOR 450 RIVER ROAD

Zoning Board Administrator Marcus T. Webb introduced the variance request for 450 River Road. The applicant, Mr. James P. Henderson, is seeking a variance to allow the construction of a two-story accessory dwelling unit (ADU) and a detached garage structure that exceeds the current setback requirements by 4.5 feet. The proposed structure is to be used for short-term rental purposes.

The applicant presented the case, highlighting the property's location in a high-demand area for short-term rentals and the architectural design of the proposed structure. The applicant submitted a traffic impact study, an architectural rendering, and a letter of support from the immediate neighbors.

The Planning Director presented the staff report, recommending the denial of the variance request. The staff report noted that the proposed setback variance of 4.5 feet does not meet the criteria for a variance under Section 14.05 of the Millbrook Zoning Ordinance. The staff argued that the property is not unique in its physical characteristics and that the hardship is self-imposed. Furthermore, the staff noted that the proposed use as a short-term rental may create a nuisance for the surrounding residential area.

Public Comment:
Mr. James P. Henderson, the applicant, spoke in support of the variance. He stated that the variance is necessary to maximize the utility of the property and that the structure will not negatively impact the character of the neighborhood. He emphasized the economic benefits of the short-term rental and the architectural design of the proposed structure.
Ms. John D. Smith, a resident of 450 River Road, spoke against the variance. She expressed concern about the potential increase in traffic and noise. She requested that the Board deny the variance.
Ms. Sarah L. Johnson, the Vice Chairperson, asked the applicant if he had addressed the noise concerns raised in the traffic impact study.
Mr. Henderson responded that he had proposed a sound barrier wall along the property line, which is included in the architectural rendering.
Member Davis asked if the neighbors had signed the letter of support.
Mr. Henderson confirmed that the letter of support was signed by the owners of 448 and 452 River Road.

Board Discussion:
Chairperson Miller asked the Board members to consider the criteria for a variance under Section 14.05 of the Millbrook Zoning Ordinance.
Vice Chairperson Johnson noted that the property is not unique in its physical characteristics.
Member Smith noted that the hardship is self-imposed.
Member Brown noted that the proposed use as a short-term rental may create a nuisance for the surrounding residential area.
Member Davis noted that the applicant has proposed a sound barrier wall, but the variance is still not justified.

Motion by Member Smith, seconded by Member Brown, to deny the variance request for 450 River Road.

Roll Call Vote:
Chairperson Miller: Aye
Vice Chairperson Johnson: Aye
Member Smith: Aye
Member Brown: Aye
Member Davis: Aye

Motion Carried.

INTER-DEPARTMENTAL MEMO
DATE: JUNE 4, 2024
TO: ALL DEPARTMENT HEADS
FROM: Richard D. Sterling, City Manager
SUBJECT: COORDINATION OF SUMMER EVENTS

This memo outlines the responsibilities of various departments for the upcoming summer festival, the farmers market, and the outdoor concert series.

DEPARTMENTAL RESPONSIBILITIES:
1. Public Works Department: Responsible for setting up stages, lighting, and sound equipment.
2. Police Department: Responsible for security, traffic control, and emergency response.
3. Parks and Recreation Department: Responsible for vendor coordination, food service, and activity planning.
4. Planning Department: Responsible for permit issuance and zoning compliance.
5. Finance Department: Responsible for budget oversight and revenue collection.
6. Communications Department: Responsible for public relations and media outreach.

The memo emphasizes the need for close coordination between departments to ensure the success of the events. A joint planning meeting is scheduled for June 10, 2024, to finalize the details.

BUDGET ALLOCATION: $150,000 (Funded by the City's Special Events Fund).
CONTINGENCY PLAN: Availability of indoor venues and postponement of events in case of severe weather.
VENDOR SELECTION PROCESS: Competitive bidding process, with priority given to local businesses.

NOTES:
1. The joint planning meeting is scheduled for June 10, 2024.
2. The budget is $150,000.
3. The contingency plan includes the availability of indoor venues and the postponement of events in case of severe weather.
4. The vendor selection process is a competitive bidding process, with priority given to local businesses.

SECTION 3: ZONING COMPLIANCE VERIFICATION MEMO

DEPARTMENT OF PLANNING AND ZONING
CITY OF MILLBROOK
DATE: June 10, 2024
TO: Office of the County Recorder
FROM: Zoning Compliance Officer
RE: Zoning Verification - Parcel 14-05-22-101

This memo serves to confirm the zoning classification and land use compliance for the property located at 1242 Oak Street, Parcel ID 14-05-22-101. The subject property is currently zoned R-1, which designates Single Family Residential use.

The zoning classification R-1 permits the following uses:
1.  Single-family dwelling units.
2.  Home offices (subject to square footage restrictions).
3.  Accessory structures (garages, sheds) not exceeding 1,200 square feet.
4.  Public utilities and infrastructure necessary for residential service.

The property transfer recorded on June 7, 2024, does not require a zoning variance or special use permit. The lot size of 10,890 sq ft meets the minimum frontage requirements for the R-1 district. The acreage of 0.25 acres is consistent with the established density for this sector of the township.

A review of the Millbrook Zoning Code, Section 14.02, indicates that the transfer of ownership does not alter the permitted use of the land. The Grantee, Thompson Residential LLC, has agreed to abide by all covenants associated with the property, including the HOA Agreement.

The survey conducted by Millbrook Surveying Inc on May 15, 2024, confirms that the physical boundaries align with the recorded plat. There are no encroachments or boundary disputes noted in the survey report. The standard utility easement noted in the deed is consistent with existing infrastructure maps held by the Department of Public Works.

No additional zoning approval is required for this transfer. The property remains in compliance with the comprehensive plan for the year 2024.

DOCUMENT 1: PUBLIC NOTICE OF ZONING BOARD OF APPEALS MEETING
-----------------------------------------------------------------------

NOTICE IS HEREBY GIVEN THAT THE ZONING BOARD OF APPEALS OF THE CITY OF OAKHAVEN WILL HOLD A REGULAR MEETING ON TUESDAY, JUNE 10, 2024, AT 6:00 P.M. IN THE CITY COUNCIL CHAMBERS LOCATED AT 101 MUNICIPAL PLAZA, SUITE 200.

THE MEETING WILL BE CONDUCTED IN PERSON AND MAY ALSO BE VIEWED VIA LIVE STREAM ON THE CITY WEBSITE. AGENDA ITEMS INCLUDE THE REVIEW OF APPLICATIONS FOR NEW CONSTRUCTION PROJECTS, VARIANCE REQUESTS, AND ADMINISTRATIVE MATTERS RELATED TO THE DEPARTMENT OF BUILDING AND ZONING.

THE FOLLOWING ITEMS ARE SCHEDULED FOR DISCUSSION:
ITEM 1: APPROVAL OF MINUTES FROM PREVIOUS MEETING
ITEM 2: PUBLIC HEARINGS AND CONSIDERATIONS
ITEM 3: ADMINISTRATIVE REPORTS FROM THE BUILDING DEPARTMENT
ITEM 4: PUBLIC COMMENT PERIOD
ITEM 5: ADJOURNMENT

ANY PERSON WHO WISHES TO APPEAR AND PRESENT EVIDENCE OR ARGUMENTS RELATING TO ANY ITEM ON THE AGENDA MAY DO SO DURING THE PUBLIC COMMENT PERIOD. WRITTEN COMMENTS MAY BE SUBMITTED TO THE OFFICE OF THE CLERK PRIOR TO THE MEETING OR HAND DELIVERED TO THE CHAIRPERSON DURING THE SESSION.

ALL RECORDS AND DOCUMENTS RELATED TO THE ITEMS ON THIS AGENDA ARE AVAILABLE FOR PUBLIC INSPECTION AT THE OFFICE OF THE CLERK, ROOM 101, DURING BUSINESS HOURS FROM 8:00 A.M. TO 5:00 P.M.

CALL TO ORDER
THE MEETING WAS CALLED TO ORDER BY CHAIRPERSON JAMES HENDERSON AT 6:05 P.M. THE CHAIRPERSON ANNOUNCED THAT A QUORUM WAS PRESENT. THE ROLL CALL WAS CONDUCTED AS FOLLOWS:

APPROVAL OF PREVIOUS MINUTES
MEMBER WILLIAMS MOVED TO APPROVE THE MINUTES OF THE PREVIOUS MEETING HELD ON MAY 13, 2024. MEMBER GARCIA SECONDED THE MOTION. UPON A SHOW OF HANDS, THE MOTION PASSED UNANIMOUSLY. THE CLERK NOTED THAT THE MINUTES WERE CORRECT AND ACCURATE REPRESENTATIONS OF THE PROCEEDINGS.

AGENDA ITEM 1: ADMINISTRATIVE REPORTS
THE BUILDING DEPARTMENT STAFF PRESENTED AN UPDATE ON CURRENT PERMITTING VOLUMES. THE STAFF REPORTED THAT APPLICATIONS SUBMITTED FOR THE MONTH OF MAY TOTALLED ONE HUNDRED AND FIFTY. THE FIRE MARSHAL INDICATED THAT NO CODE VIOLATIONS WERE NOTED DURING THE FIELD INSPECTIONS CONDUCTED DURING THE PREVIOUS WEEK.

AGENDA ITEM 2: NEW CONSTRUCTION PERMIT APPLICATION BP-2024-450
THE CHAIRPERSON CALLED AGENDA ITEM 2 TO THE FLOOR. THE APPLICANT REPRESENTED CONSTRUCTION CORP. THE OWNER OF THE PROPERTY, ELIZABETH MOORE, WAS PRESENT TO ANSWER QUESTIONS. THE PROJECT NAME IS LISTED AS ELM STREET ADDITION. THE ADDRESS IS 1245 ELM STREET, OAKHAVEN, STATE.

THE CITY ENGINEER PRESENTED THE TECHNICAL REVIEW SUMMARY. THE ENGINEER NOTED THAT THE APPLICATION MEETS ALL CURRENT CODE REQUIREMENTS. THE BUILDING DEPARTMENT STAFF CONFIRMED THAT THE PLANS WERE REVIEWED IN ACCORDANCE WITH THE 2021 IECC ENERGY CODE. THE FIRE RATING IS LISTED AS STANDARD. THE ZONING DISTRICT IS R-1. THE SETBACK COMPLIANCE WAS VERIFIED AS YES. THE HEIGHT LIMIT FOR THE DISTRICT IS 35 FEET. THE CURRENT HEIGHT OF THE PROPOSED STRUCTURE IS 30 FEET.

MEMBER THOMPSON ASKED IF THE UTILITY CONNECTIONS WERE CONFIRMED. THE BUILDING DEPARTMENT STAFF REPLIED THAT THE UTILITY CONNECTION IS CITY WATER AND THE SEWER CONNECTION IS CITY SEWER. THE FOUNDATION TYPE IS LISTED AS CONCRETE SLAB. THE ROOFING MATERIAL IS ASPHALT SHINGLE. THE SQUARE FOOTAGE IS 800 SQ FT. THE NUMBER OF STORIES IS 1. THE ESTIMATED COST IS $125,000.

MEMBER GARCIA ASKED ABOUT THE INSPECTION SCHEDULE. THE BUILDING DEPARTMENT STAFF STATED THAT THE INSPECTION SCHEDULED FOR NEXT WEEK. THE INSPECTOR ASSIGNED IS MIKE BROWN. THE PERMIT NUMBER WAS ASSIGNED SEQUENTIALLY. THE EXPIRATION DATE IS SET FOR 180 DAYS.

MEMBER WILLIAMS MOVED TO APPROVE THE PERMIT APPLICATION BP-2024-450. MEMBER CHEN SECONDED THE MOTION. THE MOTION TO GRANT APPROVAL WAS PUT TO A VOTE. THE ROLL CALL VOTE WAS AS FOLLOWS:

AGENDA ITEM 3: VARIANCE REQUESTS
NO VARIANCE REQUESTS WERE PRESENTED FOR THIS SESSION. THE CHAIRPERSON ANNOUNCED THAT ANY FUTURE REQUESTS MUST BE SUBMITTED IN WRITING AT LEAST TEN DAYS PRIOR TO THE NEXT MEETING.

THE CHAIRPERSON OPENED THE FLOOR TO PUBLIC COMMENT. ONE MEMBER OF THE PUBLIC STOOD TO SPEAK. THE MEMBER WAS IDENTIFIED AS A RESIDENT OF THE ELM STREET NEIGHBORHOOD. THE MEMBER SUBMITTED A WRITTEN COMMENT REGARDING THE PROPOSED ADDITION AT 1245 ELM STREET. THE WRITTEN COMMENT WAS FILED WITH THE CLERK. NO OTHER MEMBERS OF THE PUBLIC REQUESTED TO SPEAK.

AGENDA ITEM 5: ADMINISTRATIVE MATTERS
THE CLERK ANNOUNCED THE NEXT REGULAR MEETING IS SCHEDULED FOR JULY 8, 2024. THE CHAIRPERSON NOTED THAT THE PERMIT FEES FOR THE APPROVED APPLICATION MUST BE PAID WITHIN THIRTY DAYS TO MAINTAIN THE ACTIVE STATUS OF THE PERMIT. THE TOTAL FEES FOR THE APPLICATION WERE ASSESSED AT $1,500.00.

ADJOURNMENT
MEMBER THOMPSON MOVED TO ADJOURN THE MEETING. MEMBER WILLIAMS SECONDED THE MOTION. THE MEETING WAS ADJOURNED AT 7:15 P.M.

CITY OF OAKHAVEN
ZONING BOARD OF APPEALS
REGULAR MEETING MINUTES

**MEETING ID:** ZBA-2024-06-13
**DATE:** June 13, 2024
**LOCATION:** City Hall, Room 204, 100 Municipal Drive, Oakhaven, CA 90210
**TIME:** 6:00 PM – 7:15 PM
**SUBJECT:** Regular Meeting Minutes - June 13, 2024

**I. CALL TO ORDER AND ROLL CALL**

The Zoning Board of Appeals (hereinafter referred to as "the Board") of the City of Oakhaven convened in regular session on the 13th day of June, 2024, at 6:00 PM. The meeting was held in Room 204 of City Hall, pursuant to the provisions of the Municipal Code and the City's administrative procedures for public hearings and quasi-judicial proceedings. The meeting was webcast via the City's official digital platform, and a recording was made for archival purposes.

Board members present at the commencement of the meeting were:
1.  Robert Thompson, Chair
2.  Linda Martinez, Vice-Chair
3.  James Wilson, Member
4.  Sarah Chen, Member
5.  David Rivera, Member
6.  Elena Kowalski, Member

Absent: None.

The Chair, Robert Thompson, called the meeting to order at 6:00 PM. A roll call was taken to establish a quorum. Each member present affirmed their attendance and eligibility to vote. The Chair declared that a quorum was established at 6:02 PM, comprising six (6) of the seven (7) authorized members, satisfying the requirements of Section 17.4.2 of the Oakhaven Municipal Code.

The Chair announced the availability of the meeting agenda, which had been posted at the City Clerk's office and on the municipal website for a period of ten (10) days prior to the meeting, in compliance with the Brown Act and local open meeting laws. The agenda included the review of variance request 24-005, the approval of the minutes from the previous meeting held on May 30, 2024, and the scheduling of future hearings.

**II. APPROVAL OF MINUTES**

The Board proceeded to consider the minutes from the previous regular meeting held on May 30, 2024. The minutes had been distributed to all board members via electronic mail on June 5, 2024, and were made available for public review at the City Clerk's office.

No corrections, amendments, or additions were offered by any board member or member of the public during the review period.

Upon motion by Member Sarah Chen, seconded by Member James Wilson, the Board voted to approve the minutes of the May 30, 2024, meeting as submitted.

**Roll Call Vote on Approval of Minutes:**
*   **Robert Thompson:** Yes
*   **Linda Martinez:** Yes
*   **James Wilson:** Yes
*   **Sarah Chen:** Yes
*   **David Rivera:** Yes
*   **Elena Kowalski:** Yes

**Result:** Motion Carried (6-0).

The minutes were officially entered into the record.

**III. STAFF REPORTS AND PRESENTATIONS**

The Board received the regular staff report from the Office of Planning and Zoning. Staff Director, Marcus Patterson, was present to present the findings regarding the pending variance request. Mr. Patterson is the primary case manager assigned to the file and has been responsible for the preliminary review of the application materials.

Mr. Patterson reviewed the administrative file for Variance Request 24-005. The file included the initial application submitted by the applicant, Green Valley Farms, dated April 2, 2024. The file contained the completed application forms, the required filing fee of $450.00, and a comprehensive set of supporting documentation.

Mr. Patterson noted that the property in question is located at 890 Pine Lane, within the R-1 (Single Family Residential) zoning district. The subject property is a standard residential lot with a frontage of 60 feet and a depth of 120 feet. The request pertains to a proposed fence structure along the rear and side property lines.

The Staff Report detailed the specific parameters of the request. The applicant, Green Valley Farms, is seeking a variance to the height restriction provisions of the Oakhaven Municipal Code, specifically Section 15.2.4, which mandates a maximum fence height of 6 feet in the rear yard and 4 feet in the front yard for residential properties. The applicant is requesting permission to construct a fence with a maximum height of 8 feet.

The Staff Report indicated that the primary justification provided by the applicant for this variance is the need for enhanced privacy and security. The applicant stated that the property is situated adjacent to a busy thoroughfare and that the existing 6-foot fence does not provide sufficient visual screening from the road or adequate security for the livestock and equipment stored on the premises.

Mr. Patterson presented the findings of the site analysis. He reviewed the site plan attached to the application, which was prepared by a licensed civil engineer and bears the seal of the firm of Henderson & Associates. The site plan clearly delineated the property lines, the proposed location of the fence, and the dimensions of the structure. The plan indicated that the 8-foot fence would be constructed along the entire rear property line and the southern side property line, utilizing a solid wood construction with a privacy slat design.

Regarding the impact on neighboring properties, Mr. Patterson highlighted the correspondence on file. The neighboring property at 892 Pine Lane, owned by a private individual, had provided written consent to the proposed variance. A copy of this consent letter, dated May 15, 2024, was included in the administrative file. The letter explicitly stated that the neighbor had no objection to the increased fence height and acknowledged that the structure would not obstruct their view or light.

Mr. Patterson also reviewed the photo documentation submitted with the application. The file contained 12 digital images taken on April 1, 2024, depicting the current state of the property, the existing fencing, and the surrounding neighborhood context. These images showed a typical suburban residential street with similar lot sizes and setbacks. The photos confirmed that the proposed location for the fence would not encroach upon any public right-of-way or easements.

The Staff Report concluded with a formal recommendation. After a thorough review of the application, the site plan, the neighbor consent, and the public interest, the Planning Department staff recommended that the Board approve Variance Request 24-005. The staff noted that the request met the criteria for a variance as outlined in the Municipal Code, specifically that the variance would not alter the essential character of the neighborhood and that there was no adverse impact on surrounding properties.

Mr. Patterson stated, "The Board considered the request for variance 24-005. Staff noted no adverse impact on surrounding properties. The applicant has provided sufficient evidence to support the need for the variance, and the neighbor consent removes any potential conflict. Therefore, the staff recommendation is to approve the application."

The Chair thanked Mr. Patterson for the comprehensive report and the clear presentation of the facts. The Board members were given an opportunity to ask clarifying questions regarding the staff report.

**IV. PUBLIC COMMENT**

The Chair opened the floor for public comment regarding the items on the agenda. The Chair reminded the public that comments should be directed to the Board and that each speaker was limited to three (3) minutes.

The Chair called for public comment specifically regarding Variance Request 24-005.

The Clerk confirmed that no members of the public had signed up to speak prior to the meeting. During the public comment period, no members of the public requested the floor.

**Public Speakers:** 0

The Chair noted that the lack of public comment was consistent with the notification process, which had included a notice posted on the property and a letter sent to all abutting property owners. The Chair stated that the silence of the public did not preclude the Board from proceeding with the hearing, as the matter was fully briefed and the staff recommendation was favorable.

**V. CONSIDERATION OF VARIANCE REQUEST 24-005**

The Board proceeded to the consideration of Variance Request 24-005. The Chair called the item up for discussion.

**A. Motion and Second**

Member Linda Martinez, Vice-Chair, moved to approve Variance Request 24-005 for the property located at 890 Pine Lane, requesting a fence height variance of 8 feet in lieu of the 6-foot ordinance limit. The motion was based on the staff recommendation, the applicant's justification of privacy and security, and the consent of the neighboring property owner at 892 Pine Lane.

Member David Rivera seconded the motion.

**B. Discussion**

The Board engaged in a brief discussion regarding the motion.

Member Sarah Chen inquired about the materials proposed for the fence. Mr. Patterson responded that the site plan indicated the use of pressure-treated pine with a cedar stain, which is a standard material in the district and consistent with the aesthetic guidelines of the neighborhood.

Member Elena Kowalski asked if the fence would include any gates or driveways. Mr. Patterson confirmed that the design included a single access gate on the southern side, which would be 10 feet wide, and that the gate would operate within the property lines.

Member James Wilson raised a question regarding the drainage implications of the fence. Mr. Patterson noted that the site plan showed the fence would be installed with a 6-inch gap at the bottom to allow for surface water runoff, and that the engineer had confirmed this would not impede drainage patterns.

Member Robert Thompson, the Chair, reiterated the importance of the neighbor consent. He noted that the written consent from the property at 892 Pine Lane was a significant factor in the staff's recommendation and helped to mitigate any potential concerns regarding property boundaries or light obstruction.

**C. Voting Procedure**

The Chair called for a vote on the motion made by Member Martinez and seconded by Member Rivera. The vote was taken by roll call, as required by the Municipal Code for quasi-judicial proceedings.

**Roll Call Vote:**

1.  **Robert Thompson (Chair):** Yes
2.  **Linda Martinez (Vice-Chair):** Yes
3.  **James Wilson (Member):** Yes
4.  **Sarah Chen (Member):** Yes
5.  **David Rivera (Member):** Yes
6.  **Elena Kowalski (Member):** Yes

**Vote Summary:**
*   **Yes:** 6
*   **No:** 0
*   **Abstain:** 0

**D. Result**

The Chair declared the motion carried. The Board voted unanimously 6-0 to approve Variance Request 24-005.

The Chair stated, "Motion to approve was made by Member Martinez. Seconded by Member Rivera. Vote was unanimous 6-0."

The Board found that the request met the necessary criteria for a variance. The Board determined that the requested height of 8 feet was reasonable given the specific circumstances of the property, including the need for privacy and security, and that the construction would not adversely affect the health, safety, or welfare of the surrounding neighborhood. The Board further found that the neighbor consent provided by the owner of 892 Pine Lane confirmed that the variance would not cause hardship or nuisance to adjacent properties.

**VI. ORDER OF APPROVAL**

Pursuant to the vote, the Board directed the City Clerk to issue an Order of Approval for Variance Request 24-005. The Order was to be signed by the Chair and the City Clerk on the same day as the meeting.

**Order Details:**
*   **Order Number:** ORD-2024-13
*   **Date of Signing:** June 13, 2024
*   **Property Address:** 890 Pine Lane
*   **Applicant:** Green Valley Farms
*   **Variance Request:** 24-005
*   **Approval:** Fence Height Variance (8 feet)
*   **Distribution:** Applicant, City Clerk, Planning Department

The Order was prepared by the City Clerk's office and signed by Chair Robert Thompson and City Clerk Linda Martinez at 7:10 PM. The Order was immediately filed with the City Clerk's office and made available for public inspection. A copy of the Order was mailed to the applicant, Green Valley Farms, via first-class mail.

**VII. ADJOURNMENT**

The Chair called for a motion to adjourn the meeting.

Member Elena Kowalski moved to adjourn the meeting.

Member David Rivera seconded the motion.

The motion was approved by unanimous consent.

**Meeting End:** 7:15 PM

The Chair declared the meeting adjourned at 7:15 PM. The next regular meeting of the Zoning Board of Appeals is scheduled for June 27, 2024, at 6:00 PM in City Hall Room 204. The meeting was recorded and will be archived in the City Clerk's office.

**Type of Variance:** Area Variance (Height)
**Ordinance Section:** Section 15.2.4 (Fence Height Limits)
**Current Ordinance Limit:** 6 feet (Rear Yard)
**Requested Variance:** 8 feet
**Variance Justification:** Privacy and Security
**Description of Request:** The applicant requests a variance to the maximum fence height restriction to allow for the construction of an 8-foot tall fence along the rear and southern side property lines. The existing ordinance limits the height to 6 feet. The applicant states that the additional 2 feet is necessary to provide adequate privacy from the adjacent thoroughfare and to secure the livestock and equipment stored on the property.

**SECTION 4: SUPPORTING DOCUMENTATION**

The following documents were submitted with this application:

1.  **Application Fee:** $450.00 (Paid via Check #45022)

4.  **Neighbor Consent:** Written consent letter from the owner of 892 Pine Lane, dated May 15, 2024.
5.  **Architectural Summary:** Description of fence materials (Pressure-treated pine, cedar stain) and design (Privacy slat).
6.  **Drainage Plan:** Confirmation from engineer that the 6-inch bottom gap will not impede drainage.

**SECTION 5: NOTIFICATION**

**Method of Notification:**
-   Notice posted on property: Yes
-   Letter mailed to abutting owners: Yes
-   Publication in local newspaper: No
-   Webcast notification: Yes

**Abutting Property Owners Notified:**

2.  888 Pine Lane (Owner: Private Individual)
3.  894 Pine Lane (Owner: Private Individual)

**SECTION 6: STAFF REVIEW AND RECOMMENDATION**

**Staff Reviewer:** Marcus Patterson, Senior Planner
**Review Date:** June 5, 2024
**Staff Recommendation:** APPROVE

**Staff Findings:**
1.  **Hardship:** The applicant has demonstrated a specific hardship related to privacy and security that is unique to the property due to its location adjacent to a high-traffic area.
2.  **No Adverse Impact:** The variance will not alter the essential character of the neighborhood. The proposed height is consistent with the scale of the surrounding structures.
3.  **Neighbor Consent:** Consent has been obtained from the neighboring property at 892 Pine Lane, mitigating any potential conflicts.
4.  **Compliance:** The proposed fence design complies with all other zoning regulations, including setbacks and material restrictions.

**SECTION 7: BOARD ACTION**

**Date of Decision:** June 13, 2024
**Board Action:** Approved
**Vote Count:** 6-0 (Unanimous)
**Order Number:** ORD-2024-13
**Effective Date:** June 13, 2024

**APPROVAL CONDITIONS:**
1.  The fence must be constructed in accordance with the site plan submitted with this application.
2.  The fence must not encroach upon any public right-of-way or easement.
3.  The fence must be maintained in good repair by the property owner.
4.  The variance applies only to the specific property at 890 Pine Lane and does not set a precedent for other properties.

**SECTION 8: APPEAL RIGHTS**

Any party aggrieved by this decision may appeal to the City Council within 15 days of the date of this decision. The appeal must be filed in writing with the City Clerk.

**CITY OF OAKHAVEN**
**PUBLIC COMMENT SUBMISSION**
**SUBMISSION ID:** PC-2024-06-13-001
**DATE SUBMITTED:** June 13, 2024
**TIME:** 5:45 PM
**SUBMISSION METHOD:** Electronic Form (City Website)
**STATUS:** Received

**COMPLAINANT INFORMATION**

**Name:** John Doe
**Address:** 888 Pine Lane, Oakhaven, CA 90210
**Email:** j.doe@oakhaven.com
**Phone:** (555) 555-0100
**Relationship to Property:** Abutting Neighbor

**SUBMISSION DETAILS**

**Subject:** Public Comment on Variance Request 24-005
**Property Address:** 890 Pine Lane
**Hearing Date:** June 13, 2024

**Comment Text:**
I am writing to express my concern regarding the proposed variance for the property at 890 Pine Lane. While I understand the applicant's need for privacy, I believe that an 8-foot fence may still present issues regarding wind flow and drainage in the rear yard area. I have observed that the existing 6-foot fence on my property has caused some water pooling during heavy rains, and I am concerned that increasing the height of the fence on the adjacent property may exacerbate this issue.

Furthermore, I am concerned that the 8-foot fence may block the view of the street from my residence, which could be a safety concern for pedestrians and vehicles. I request that the Board consider the potential impact on the microclimate of the neighborhood and the safety of the public right-of-way.

I am not opposed to the concept of a fence, but I urge the Board to ensure that the design includes adequate drainage solutions and that the height is not excessive. I would appreciate it if the Board could review the drainage plan submitted by the applicant to ensure that no adverse effects will occur.

I am willing to attend the meeting to discuss this matter further if required.

**SECTION: RESPONSE FROM STAFF**

**Response Date:** June 13, 2024
**Respondent:** Marcus Patterson, Senior Planner
**Response Text:**
The staff has reviewed the public comment submitted by John Doe. The site plan submitted with the variance application includes a drainage analysis which indicates that the proposed fence will not impede water flow. The engineer has confirmed that the 6-inch gap at the bottom of the fence will allow for adequate drainage. Regarding the view obstruction, the staff notes that the variance is for a rear yard fence, which does not typically impact the view of the street from the front yard. The staff recommends that the Board proceed with the approval as the technical details have been addressed in the application.

**CITY OF OAKHAVEN**
**ENVIRONMENTAL REVIEW SUMMARY**
**PROJECT:** Variance Request 24-005 (Fence Height)
**LOCATION:** 890 Pine Lane, Oakhaven, CA 90210
**DATE:** June 13, 2024
**REVIEWER:** Environmental Compliance Office
**STATUS:** Exempt from CEQA Review

**SECTION 1: PROJECT DESCRIPTION**

The proposed project involves the construction of an 8-foot tall wooden fence along the rear and southern side property lines of the residential property located at 890 Pine Lane. The fence will be constructed of pressure-treated pine with a cedar stain. The project is classified as a minor land use action within the R-1 zoning district.

**SECTION 2: CEQA DETERMINATION**

Pursuant to the California Environmental Quality Act (CEQA), the City of Oakhaven has determined that the proposed project is exempt from environmental review. The project falls under the following categorical exemption:
*   **Exemption Category:** Class 1 (Existing Facilities)
*   **Exemption Criteria:** The project involves the construction of a fence on an existing residential lot. The project does not involve any significant expansion of the lot, nor does it involve the construction of any new structures that would alter the physical environment.

**SECTION 3: IMPACT ANALYSIS**

**Air Quality:** No impact. The construction of a wooden fence does not involve heavy machinery or emissions-generating activities.
**Water Quality:** No impact. The project does not involve any work in or near water bodies. The drainage plan confirms that surface water runoff will not be impeded.
**Biological Resources:** No impact. The project is located within an urban residential area. No sensitive species or habitats are present on the property.
**Noise:** No impact. Construction noise will be temporary and limited to standard daylight hours.
**Traffic:** No impact. The project does not generate additional traffic.
**Land Use:** No impact. The project is consistent with the zoning regulations and the general plan.

**SECTION 4: CONCLUSION**

Based on the review, the Environmental Compliance Office concludes that the proposed project will not have a significant effect on the environment. The project is exempt from further environmental review.

The department prepared the staff report and presented the findings to the Board.

**7. LEGAL DEPARTMENT**
The Legal Department reviewed the variance request and the staff report to ensure that the Board's decision was consistent with the Municipal Code and state law. They confirmed that the Board's action was within its authority.

**CONCLUSION**
All departments have provided the necessary support to ensure that the variance request 24-005 was processed in a timely and efficient manner. The Board's decision to approve the request was made in accordance with all applicable laws and regulations.

CITY OF MILLBROOK
ZONING BOARD OF APPEALS
MEETING MINUTES

DATE: June 10, 2024
TIME: 18:00
LOCATION: Municipal Building, Council Chamber 1
CHAIRPERSON: Robert J. Miller
SECRETARY: Sarah L. Jenkins

ROLL CALL

The following members were present:
1. Robert J. Miller (Chairperson)
2. Elena G. Vasquez (Vice Chairperson)
3. David T. Chen
4. Marcus A. Williams
5. Jennifer L. Thompson

The following members were absent:
1. Thomas R. Harris (Excused)
2. Susan K. O’Malley (Excused)

A quorum was present. The meeting was called to order at 18:05.

OPENING REMARKS

Chairperson Miller called the meeting to order. The Chairperson stated that the meeting would follow the agenda adopted by the Board at the previous session. The Chairperson reminded members of the open meeting laws and the requirement for public comment.

AGENDA ITEM 1: PUBLIC COMMENT

The floor was opened for public comment. Three members of the public addressed the Board.

1. Member 1: Mr. James A. Roberts. Mr. Roberts expressed concern regarding traffic patterns associated with proposed developments in the Industrial District.
2. Member 2: Ms. Patricia K. Green. Ms. Green requested clarification on setback requirements for new commercial structures.
3. Member 3: Mr. Daniel S. Wright. Mr. Wright addressed noise mitigation standards for industrial zones.

The Secretary recorded the comments. The Board acknowledged the comments for the record.

AGENDA ITEM 2: VARIANCE REQUEST – LAKESIDE PROPERTIES GROUP

Case Number: ZBA-2024-042
Applicant: Lakeside Properties Group
Property Address: 500 Industrial Way
Property Owner: Lakeside Properties Group
Representative: Mr. Alan S. Mitchell

The Applicant requested a variance from Section 14.2.4 of the Municipal Code regarding frontage requirements. The proposed structure requires a reduction in frontage width to accommodate existing utility easements.

MOTION 1

Mr. Chen moved to approve the variance request.
Second: Mr. Williams.

Discussion:
Mr. Miller stated the Board must consider the hardship presented by the existing easements.
Ms. Vasquez inquired about the impact on neighboring properties.
Mr. Mitchell stated there would be no impact on neighboring properties.
Mr. Chen stated the variance is consistent with the comprehensive plan.
Mr. Williams stated the variance meets the criteria for relief.

Vote Count:
Yes: 5
No: 0
Abstain: 0

Motion 1 Passed.

AGENDA ITEM 3: PERMIT REVIEW – GREENFIELD DEVELOPMENT PARTNERS

Case Number: ZBA-2024-045
Applicant: Greenfield Development Partners
Property Address: 800 Commerce Drive
Property Owner: Greenfield Development Partners
Representative: Ms. Linda K. Foster

The Applicant requested approval for a site plan amendment. The amendment concerns the location of parking spaces.

MOTION 2

Ms. Thompson moved to approve the site plan amendment.
Second: Mr. Miller.

Discussion:
Ms. Foster stated the amendment improves accessibility.
Mr. Chen noted the amendment aligns with ADA standards.
Mr. Williams noted the amendment does not exceed zoning limits.

Vote Count:
Yes: 5
No: 0
Abstain: 0

Motion 2 Passed.

AGENDA ITEM 4: DISCUSSION ON FUTURE DEVELOPMENT ZONES

The Board discussed potential rezoning of Sector 7. No action was taken. The Chairperson noted that future meetings would address the specific parcels involved.

AGENDA ITEM 5: ADJOURNMENT

There being no further business, the meeting was adjourned at 19:45.

Minutes submitted by: Sarah L. Jenkins, Secretary.
Approved by: Robert J. Miller, Chairperson.
Date of Approval: June 12, 2024.

End of Zoning Board of Appeals Meeting Minutes.

CITY OF MILLBROOK
PUBLIC COMMENT SUBMISSION
OFFICE OF THE CITY CLERK

SUBMISSION ID: PC-2024-06-14-003
DATE: June 14, 2024
SUBMITTED BY: Community Action Group
ADDRESS: 200 Oak Street, Millbrook
CONTACT: info@communityaction.org
PHONE: (555) 019-5555

SUBJECT: Objection to Zoning Variance Request

TO: Zoning Board of Appeals
ATTN: Robert J. Miller, Chairperson

Dear Chairperson Miller and Members of the Board,

The Community Action Group submits this formal comment regarding the variance request filed by Lakeside Properties Group. The group has reviewed the documentation provided by the Applicant. The group has concerns regarding the impact on the surrounding residential area.

The group notes that the variance request seeks to reduce frontage requirements. The group believes this reduction may set a precedent for future developments. The group requests that the Board consider the cumulative impact of such reductions.

The group requests that the Board hold a public hearing on the matter. The group requests that the Board require additional environmental impact studies. The group requests that the Board require additional traffic studies.

The group believes that the current zoning code is sufficient to protect the interests of the community. The group believes that variances should be granted only in cases of extreme hardship. The group does not believe that extreme hardship exists in this case.

The group reserves the right to appeal any decision made by the Board. The group reserves the right to seek legal counsel. The group reserves the right to petition the City Council.

This comment is submitted for the public record. This comment is not an endorsement or opposition to the Applicant. This comment is an expression of concern regarding the process.

Sincerely,
Community Action Group
Board of Directors
June 14, 2024

End of Public Comment Submission.

CITY OF MILLBROOK
INTER-DEPARTMENTAL MEMORANDUM

TO: Ethics Commission
FROM: Planning and Zoning Department
DATE: June 15, 2024
SUBJECT: Notification of Campaign Finance Filing Related to Pending Zoning Matter
REFERENCE: CF-2024-06-16

SECTION 1: PURPOSE

The purpose of this memorandum is to notify the Ethics Commission of a campaign finance filing that is related to a pending zoning matter. The Planning and Zoning Department has reviewed the filing and has determined that the filing is relevant to the current zoning process.

SECTION 2: BACKGROUND

On June 12, 2024, the Ethics Commission received a campaign finance filing. The filing reported a contribution from Lakeside Properties Group. The contribution was made to the Patterson Campaign Committee and the Kowalski for ZBA committee. The contribution amount was $2,000.00 USD per committee.

The Planning and Zoning Department is currently reviewing a variance request from Lakeside Properties Group. The variance request is Case Number ZBA-2024-042. The variance request was presented at the meeting on June 10, 2024.

SECTION 3: OBSERVATIONS

The Planning and Zoning Department observes that the contribution was made prior to the final decision on the variance request. The Planning and Zoning Department observes that the contribution was made by the applicant. The Planning and Zoning Department observes that the contribution was made to a committee associated with the Zoning Board of Appeals.

SECTION 4: ACTIONS

The Planning and Zoning Department recommends that the Ethics Commission review the filing for compliance. The Planning and Zoning Department recommends that the Ethics Commission verify the donor identification. The Planning and Zoning Department recommends that the Ethics Commission verify the amount logged.

SECTION 5: RECOMMENDATIONS

The Planning and Zoning Department recommends that the Ethics Commission maintain the filing on public record. The Planning and Zoning Department recommends that the Ethics Commission disclose the filing to the public. The Planning and Zoning Department recommends that the Ethics Commission monitor future filings.

SECTION 6: CONTACT

For questions regarding this memorandum, please contact the Planning and Zoning Department.

Prepared by:
Sarah L. Jenkins
Zoning Administrator
Department of Planning and Zoning

End of Inter-departmental Memo.

EXECUTIVE SUMMARY

This memorandum serves as the official Staff Report for the upcoming Public Hearing scheduled for June 27, 2024. The subject matter concerns the application submitted by Crestview Holdings LLC for a Variance Request. The application is identified by Reference Number VAR-24-010. The property in question is located at 1500 River Road. The Parcel ID associated with this request is 14-05-22-200. The project name listed on the application is Riverfront Luxury Apartments. The proposed development includes a Unit Count of 200. The Applicant is Crestview Holdings LLC. The Principal listed on the application is Marcus Webb. The Staff Reviewer assigned to this case is James Wilson. The Zoning District for the subject property is R-1.

The Staff Review has been completed as of June 18, 2024. The application for variance 24-010 is incomplete. This determination is based on the submission of documentation that fails to satisfy the minimum requirements for a complete application under City Code Section 14.2. The application for variance 24-010 is incomplete. This determination is based on the submission of documentation that fails to satisfy the minimum requirements for a complete application under City Code Section 14.2.

The Staff Recommendation is to DENY the request. The recommendation is to DENY the request. This conclusion follows the review of the submitted plans against the established zoning ordinances. The review process included a comparison of the proposed density and parking provisions against the standards applicable to the R-1 Zoning District. The application for variance 24-010 is incomplete.

BACKGROUND AND PROJECT DESCRIPTION

The Applicant, Crestview Holdings LLC, has submitted a request for relief from specific provisions of the City Zoning Ordinance. The project name is Riverfront Luxury Apartments. The Unit Count is 200. The Applicant seeks a Variance for Density and a Variance for Parking. The Request is Variance for Density. The Request is Variance for Parking. The Zoning District is R-1. The Address is 1500 River Road. The Parcel ID is 14-05-22-200. The Date of this Memo is June 19, 2024. The Meeting Time is 6:00 PM. The Room is City Hall 204. The Agenda Item is 4. The Attachments are 15 Documents.

The surrounding area consists primarily of single-family residential structures. The proposed development represents a significant change in the built form relative to the existing neighborhood fabric. The density is incompatible with surrounding character. This observation is based on the comparison of the proposed Unit Count against the allowable density for the R-1 District. The density is incompatible with surrounding character.

TECHNICAL ANALYSIS: PARKING

The Staff Reviewer, James Wilson, conducted a detailed analysis of the parking provisions included in the application. The Parking calculations do not meet R-1 standards. The Parking calculations do not meet R-1 standards. The R-1 Zoning District requires a minimum of one parking space per residential unit, with additional spaces required for guest use and commercial components. The proposed project includes 200 units. The application proposes a total of 200 parking spaces. This calculation does not account for the required guest parking or service vehicle parking mandated by Section 12.5 of the Municipal Code. The Parking calculations do not meet R-1 standards.

The Staff Review identified that the proposed parking ratio falls below the 1.1 ratio required for multi-family developments of this size in the R-1 District. The application for variance 24-010 is incomplete. The missing documentation includes a certified traffic study that would demonstrate the impact of the additional vehicles on the surrounding street network. Without this study, the Board cannot determine the adequacy of the parking supply.

TECHNICAL ANALYSIS: DENSITY

The Staff Reviewer evaluated the proposed density of the project. The Density is incompatible with surrounding character. The Density is incompatible with surrounding character. The R-1 District is designated for low-density residential use. The standard lot coverage for R-1 Districts does not support the footprint required for a 200-unit structure. The proposed structure would exceed the maximum building height and floor area ratio allowed under the current zoning designation. The application for variance 24-010 is incomplete.

The surrounding character is defined by single-family dwellings with significant setbacks. The proposed development would reduce the setbacks on the River Road frontage significantly. This reduction is not supported by the design guidelines for the Riverfront Corridor. The Density is incompatible with surrounding character.

TECHNICAL ANALYSIS: TRAFFIC IMPACT

The Staff Review considered the potential traffic impact of the proposed development. One of the primary reasons for the Staff Recommendation is Traffic Impact. Reason 1: Insufficient Parking. Reason 2: Density Incompatible. Reason 3: Traffic Impact. The R-1 District is not designed to accommodate the volume of vehicle trips generated by a 200-unit apartment complex. The local street network, specifically the intersection of River Road and Elm Street, operates at capacity during peak morning and evening hours. The addition of 200 new units would generate approximately 400 additional vehicle trips per day. This volume exceeds the threshold for a traffic study exemption under City Code Section 9.3.

The application for variance 24-010 is incomplete. The Traffic Impact Statement submitted with the application was dated prior to the current traffic count data collection period. The data used for the projections is outdated. The application for variance 24-010 is incomplete.

LEGAL ANALYSIS

The request for Variance is governed by Section 10.1 of the Zoning Ordinance. The Board must find that practical difficulties exist that are not applicable to the general property owner. The Staff Review found that the difficulties cited by the Applicant are self-imposed. The Applicant purchased the property with knowledge of the existing zoning restrictions. The application for variance 24-010 is incomplete.

The request for Variance does not constitute a special use permit. The request is strictly for a variance from the dimensional requirements. The Staff Recommendation is to DENY the request. The Staff Recommendation is to DENY the request. The recommendation is based on the findings that the project does not meet the criteria for a variance.

RECOMMENDATION

The Staff Recommendation is to DENY the request. The recommendation is to DENY the request. The reasons for this recommendation are summarized as follows:
Reason 1: Insufficient Parking. The proposed parking supply does not meet the minimum requirements for the R-1 District.
Reason 2: Density Incompatible. The proposed density exceeds the allowable limits for the surrounding character and zoning district.
Reason 3: Traffic Impact. The proposed development would create an unacceptable burden on the local street network.

The application for variance 24-010 is incomplete. The Staff Reviewer, James Wilson, recommends that the Board deny the variance request based on the findings detailed in this report. The application for variance 24-010 is incomplete.

MEETING SCHEDULE

A Public Hearing has been scheduled to consider this matter. The Public Hearing Date is June 27, 2024. The Meeting Time is 6:00 PM. The Room is City Hall 204. The Agenda Item is 4. The meeting is open to the public. Members of the public may submit comments prior to the hearing or during the designated public comment period. The Public Hearing Date is June 27, 2024.

SECTION 12.5: PARKING STANDARDS
R-1 District
12.5.1 General Requirement. All residential developments in the R-1 District shall provide one parking space for each dwelling unit.
12.5.2 Guest Parking. An additional 10% of the total number of units shall be provided for guest parking.
12.5.3 Service Parking. One space per 50 units shall be provided for service vehicles.
12.5.4 Variance. A variance may be granted if the Board finds that the standard is not practical.
12.5.5 Incomplete Application. The application for variance 24-010 is incomplete.

SECTION 14.2: DENSITY STANDARDS
R-1 District
14.2.1 Maximum Density. The maximum density for the R-1 District is 4 units per acre.
14.2.2 Floor Area Ratio. The maximum Floor Area Ratio is 0.4.
14.2.3 Building Height. The maximum building height is 35 feet.
14.2.4 Density Incompatible. The Density is incompatible with surrounding character.

SECTION 9.3: TRAFFIC IMPACT
9.3.1 Threshold. Projects generating more than 300 new trips per day require a traffic study.
9.3.2 Exemption. Projects generating fewer than 300 trips per day are exempt.
9.3.3 Study Requirements. The study must be prepared by a licensed traffic engineer.
9.3.4 Data Validity. Data must be collected within 12 months of the application date.

This memorandum serves to formally transmit the attached public records relating to zoning applications and property transfers processed on June 22, 2024. The documents contained within this bundle include property transfer records, zoning board meeting minutes, staff reports, and public comment logs. All documents are filed in compliance with the City of Oakhaven Municipal Code Chapter 12 regarding Public Access to Information.

The bundle contains records associated with Parcel 14-05-22-202 located at 1504 River Road. A warranty deed was recorded on the aforementioned date. The transaction involved a change of ownership from Alice Brown to Summit Point Capital. Millbrook Title Company acted as the closing agent for this transaction. The transfer tax and recording fees have been verified as paid in full.

The bundle also contains the official minutes for the Zoning Board of Appeals Regular Meeting held on June 22, 2024. The meeting address was City Hall Room 204. The meeting commenced at 6:00 PM and concluded at 7:30 PM. A quorum was established for the session. The Board reviewed the Crestview Holdings application regarding Variance Request 24-010. The applicant sought a variance for density at the property address 1500 River Road. Staff recommendation was to deny the request based on insufficient parking documentation. Three public speakers provided testimony during the public comment period. The Board voted to deny the variance. The Order was signed on June 22, 2024, with Order Number ORD-2024-22.

Distribution of these records shall be made to the Applicant, City Clerk, and the Public Record. The archival status is marked as Complete. The next scheduled meeting is set for June 27, 2024. Webcast availability is confirmed for this session. Contact information for the ZBA Clerk is available in the contact appendix. The recorder for this session was the Administrative Assistant. The transcript is pending finalization. Audio and video files have been recorded and stored in the secure archive.

This bundle is generated automatically through the Municipal Records Management System. All digital copies are available online. Physical copies are filed in Room 101. The record status is Official. The timestamp for the final recording of the property deed was 16:00:00 EST. The document index is Book 2024-12, Page 452. The acquisition timeline for the property transfer was June 19-22. A price discrepancy was noted during the initial valuation review.

SECTION 2: STAFF REPORT SUMMARY
STAFF REPORT ID: PD-2024-06-19
AUTHOR: PLANNING DEPARTMENT STAFF
DATE: JUNE 19, 2024
SUBJECT: PRELIMINARY REVIEW OF VARIANCE REQUEST 24-010

This staff report summarizes the preliminary review of the application submitted by Crestview Holdings LLC. The application was received on June 1, 2024. The request type is Variance for Density. The property address is 1500 River Road. The applicant seeks to increase the allowable density on the lot beyond the current zoning restrictions. The current zoning allows for a maximum of 10 units per acre. The applicant requests a variance to allow for 15 units per acre.

The staff reviewed the parking documentation submitted with the application. The documentation provided was incomplete. The applicant failed to provide the required parking space calculations for the additional units. The staff noted insufficient parking documentation. The current zoning code requires 1.5 parking spaces per unit. The applicant proposed 1.2 parking spaces per unit. This proposal does not meet the minimum requirement.

The site plan shows the proposed building footprint. The building footprint covers 60% of the lot area. The maximum allowed coverage is 50%. The applicant requested a variance for lot coverage as well. The site plan shows a setback of 5 feet from the property line. The required setback is 10 feet. The applicant requested a variance for the setback requirement.

The environmental review indicates no impact on flood zones. The property is not located within a floodplain. The soil report indicates stable soil conditions. The geotechnical report was submitted on May 30, 2024. The report number is GEOT-2024-0530-02. The report was reviewed by the Engineering Department. The Engineering Department has no objections to the structural design. The Planning Department has objections regarding the parking and density.

The applicant was notified of the deficiencies on June 10, 2024. The applicant was given 10 days to correct the documentation. The deadline for correction was June 20, 2024. The applicant did not submit the corrected documentation. The applicant response was None. The staff recommends denial of the variance request. The denial is based on the failure to meet the minimum parking requirements and the lack of supporting documentation for the lot coverage variance.

The staff recommends that the Zoning Board of Appeals vote to deny variance 24-010. The denial will maintain the current zoning standards for the area. The current standards protect the character of the neighborhood. The standards ensure adequate infrastructure capacity. The standards ensure safe pedestrian access. The standards ensure adequate sunlight for adjacent properties.

The staff report was distributed to the Zoning Board members on June 20, 2024. The members received the report via email and the online portal. The report includes the site plan, the parking calculations, and the soil report. The report is available for public inspection. The report is included in the meeting packet for the June 22, 2024 session. The staff recommends that the Board review the application at the regular meeting. The Board may request additional information from the applicant. The Board may request a public hearing. The Board may vote to approve or deny the request.

The project is classified as Project Type: Affordable Housing. The proposal includes a Unit Count: 40 residential units. The property is located within the R-1 Zoning District. The Federal Grant ID: FHA-2024-99 was cited in the application documentation as the funding source for the development.

PUBLIC COMMENT

The comments submitted by the public were noted in the official log. The Community Reaction: Strong Opposition was recorded in the staff summary based on the volume and nature of the comments received. The Media Coverage: Local News was noted as a factor in the public awareness of this hearing.

STAFF PRESENTATION AND TECHNICAL REVIEW
The Planning Department Representative presented the Staff Report ID: PD-2024-06-20. The report detailed the zoning compliance issues associated with the proposed construction. The Board considered the Land Trust application regarding the variance request.

During the technical review, the Board reviewed the setback calculations submitted with the application. It was noted that the Setback calculation was found to be non-compliant. The technicality cited was specifically regarding the Setback Calculation required by Section 5-102 of the Municipal Code. The Applicant Response: Pending was recorded regarding the request for clarification on the specific measurements.

MEMBER DISCUSSION
Member Marcus Patterson raised the issue of the setback requirements. Member Elena Kowalski inquired about the grant implications. Member David Rivera facilitated the discussion. Member Sarah Chen noted the zoning district requirements.

The Board discussed the implications of the variance request on the surrounding properties. The Grant Impact: Contingency at Risk was discussed as part of the staff briefing regarding the Federal Grant ID: FHA-2024-99. The Board acknowledged the Project Type: Affordable Housing designation.

MOTION AND SECOND
The Board moved to the vote on the variance request. Member Marcus Patterson made a motion. The Motion to deny was made by Member Patterson. Member Elena Kowalski seconded the motion. The Seconded by Member Kowalski was recorded in the minutes.

The discussion focused on the technical compliance of the setback variance. The Chairperson called for a vote on the motion.

VOTE RECORD

Member Marcus Patterson: No

Member Sarah Chen: Yes

The Vote was 4-3 to deny. The Roll Call Vote: Yes (3) was recorded. The Roll Call Vote: No (4) was recorded. The Roll Call Vote: Abstain (0) was recorded.

The Motion Carried. The denial of the variance request 24-011 was officially recorded.

This memorandum serves as formal notification of the decision rendered by the Zoning Board of Appeals during the Regular Meeting held on June 28, 2024. The decision pertains to Variance Request 24-011 submitted by Millbrook Community Land Trust.

DECISION SUMMARY
The Board voted to deny the variance request. The Vote was 4-3 to deny. The Roll Call Vote: Yes (3) was recorded. The Roll Call Vote: No (4) was recorded. The Roll Call Vote: Abstain (0) was recorded. The Motion Carried. The Order Signed: June 28, 2024. The Order Number: ORD-2024-28. The Distribution: Applicant, City Clerk. The Archival Status: Complete.

TECHNICAL DETAILS
The denial was based on the Technicality Cited: Setback Calculation. The Setback calculation was found to be non-compliant. The Board considered the Land Trust application. The Board noted that the Setback Calculation required by Section 5-102 of the Municipal Code was not met. The Board noted that the property is located at 1600 Community Drive. The Board noted that the property is in the Zoning District: R-1. The Board noted that the property is the site of a Project Type: Affordable Housing. The Board noted that the property has a Unit Count: 40. The Board noted that the property is funded by Federal Grant ID: FHA-2024-99. The Board noted that the Grant Impact: Contingency at Risk is a concern. The Board noted that the Applicant Response: Pending is recorded. The Board noted that the Appeal Deadline: July 28, 2024.
